title: Cache versus artifacts semantics
description: Cache is a best-effort speedup keyed and reused across pipelines; artifacts are guaranteed job outputs passed downstream and expirable.
body: Use when choosing how to move data between jobs. Cache is an optimization: keyed (ideally on the lockfile), shared opportunistically across pipelines and runners, and never guaranteed to be present, so a job must work on a cache miss. Artifacts are explicit outputs uploaded after a job and downloaded by downstream jobs per dependencies:/needs:, with expire_in controlling retention. Do not use cache for correctness-critical handoff or upload secrets as artifacts. Actions: cache dependency directories with a lockfile key; use artifacts for build outputs and reports with scoped paths and expiry. Validation signals include builds succeeding on cache miss and downstream jobs receiving only requested artifacts. Risk boundary: artifacts capturing a credential file leak it to anyone with pipeline access.
